title: neolaugeria hotteana
intro: Neolaugeria hotteana, also known as Hotte's laurel, is a shrub native to the Hawaiian Islands. The plant contains medicinal properties, including saponins and flavonoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Neolaugeria hotteana has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and sore throat, as well as to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Neolaugeria hotteana can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
